conditions.description: Diffuse fatty infiltration of the liver parenchyma. Associated with metabolic syndrome, obesity, and insulin resistance. Reversible with diet and weight reduction.
2mm calculus in the lower pole of the right kidney with no evidence of obstruction or hydronephrosis. Below threshold for intervention — hydration and periodic monitoring recommended.
Early calcific deposits in the aortic wall occurring sooner than expected for age. Associated with elevated cardiovascular risk — lipid management and blood pressure monitoring advised.
